name: scomp-link
description: End-to-end ML toolkit: 15 MCP tools for zero-code machine learning. Train models, tune hyperparameters, detect drift, generate reports, forecast time series, detect anomalies, and check fairness.

tools.name: describe_data
tools.description: Profile a dataset. Returns column-level stats: dtype, missing%, unique count, min, max, mean, std.

tools.name: train_model
tools.description: Train an ML model. Supports regression, classification, text, clustering. Set engineer=true for feature engineering, tune=true for Optuna.

tools.name: predict
tools.description: Generate predictions from a .scomp artifact on new data.

tools.name: validate_model
tools.description: Evaluate a saved model on test data. Returns metrics and optional HTML report.

tools.name: detect_drift
tools.description: Detect distribution drift between reference and current data using PSI and KS test.

tools.name: detect_anomalies
tools.description: Detect anomalies using multi-method consensus (Isolation Forest, LOF, TabNet, Transformer).

tools.name: check_fairness
tools.description: Compute fairness metrics: demographic parity, disparate impact, equalized odds.

tools.name: forecast_series
tools.description: Forecast future values of a time series. Methods: auto, arima, exp_smoothing.

tools.name: engineer_features
tools.description: Apply automated feature engineering: interactions, log transforms, date extraction, target encoding.

tools.name: cluster_data
tools.description: Cluster data using KMeans or MeanShift. Returns labels and silhouette score.

tools.name: generate_report
tools.description: Generate an interactive HTML report (EDA or model evaluation).

tools.name: create_visualization
tools.description: Create a single visualization from 39 chart types (plotly, rawgraphs, highcharts).

tools.name: compare_models
tools.description: Compare multiple .scomp artifacts side by side.

tools.name: export_model
tools.description: Export a .scomp model to pickle, joblib, or onnx format.

prompts.name: ml_workflow
prompts.description: Template for a complete ML workflow from data to deployed model.
prompts.text:
You are building an ML pipeline with scomp-link.

Dataset: ${arguments.data_path}
Target column: ${arguments.target}
Task: ${arguments.task}

Follow these steps:
1. Profile the data with describe_data
2. Check quality issues
3. Engineer features if needed
4. Train and tune the model
5. Validate on held-out test data
6. Save the artifact

prompts.name: debug_model
prompts.description: Template for diagnosing and fixing model performance issues.
prompts.text:
You are debugging a model with poor performance.

Artifact: ${arguments.artifact_path}
Issue: ${arguments.issue}

Diagnostic steps:
1. Inspect the artifact
2. Check for data drift
3. Look at feature importance
4. Check for fairness issues
5. Try feature engineering
6. Consider tuning
